title: Reading and Moving Through Text
description: Learn how to move through documents and have NVDA read text back to you. Each practice step includes a text area right inside the Coach window — no extra window needed.

steps.instruction: When your cursor is in text, the Right Arrow key moves forward one character at a time and the Left Arrow key moves backward. NVDA speaks each character as you land on it. Sighted users can see each character; NVDA announces it for you as you move. A practice text area is included right in each practice step below. Press Enter to continue.
hints: The Right and Left Arrow keys are in the arrow key cluster in the lower right area of the keyboard. Right moves forward one character; Left moves back one.
NVDA announces each character as your cursor lands on it. For punctuation, NVDA speaks the punctuation name — for example, it says 'period' instead of just making a sound.
Moving by character is useful for checking spelling, reading character by character through a word you do not recognize, or confirming what you just typed.

steps.instruction: Arrow down into the practice text area below, then press Right Arrow to move forward one character. NVDA will announce the character you land on. Try it a few times.
steps.hint: The Right Arrow key is in the arrow key cluster in the lower-right area of your keyboard.
hints: The Right Arrow key is in the arrow key cluster in the lower right of the keyboard. Press it once to move one character forward.
NVDA announces the character your cursor lands on. For letters it speaks the letter; for spaces it says 'space'; for punctuation it speaks the name.
Click into the practice text area first, or press Tab to move focus into it, then start pressing Right Arrow.
steps.successMessage: You moved forward one character. NVDA announced whatever character your cursor landed on.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Now press Left Arrow to move back one character. NVDA will announce it.
steps.hint: The Left Arrow key is to the left of the Down Arrow key.
hints: The Left Arrow key is to the left of the Down Arrow key in the arrow key cluster. Press it once to move one character backward.
NVDA speaks the character your cursor backs onto. This is the same character that was to the left of where you were.
Right and Left Arrow give you complete control over reading any word or line one character at a time — useful for proofreading or confirming spelling.
steps.successMessage: You moved back one character. Right and Left Arrow let you read any text one character at a time.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Character by character is slow when you need to scan text. Control plus Right Arrow jumps to the start of the next word. Control plus Left Arrow jumps back to the previous word. NVDA speaks the word you land on. This shortcut works the same way in Word, Notepad, email, and most other programs — with or without a screen reader. Press Enter to continue.
hints: Control plus Right Arrow jumps forward one word at a time. Hold the Control key in the bottom left corner of the keyboard and press Right Arrow.
Control plus Left Arrow jumps backward one word. NVDA speaks each word as your cursor lands on it.
Word-by-word navigation is much faster than character-by-character for scanning a sentence or finding a specific word in a paragraph.

steps.instruction: Arrow into the practice text below, then press Control+Right Arrow to jump to the next word. NVDA will announce the word you land on.
steps.hint: Hold the Control key (bottom-left of keyboard) and press the Right Arrow at the same time.
hints: Hold the Control key in the bottom left corner of the keyboard and press the Right Arrow. Both keys must be held at the same time.
NVDA announces the word your cursor jumps to. Each press moves one full word forward.
If you hold Control plus Right Arrow down, your cursor will keep jumping word by word rapidly. Release to stop.
steps.successMessage: You jumped forward one word. NVDA announced the word your cursor landed on.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Now press Control+Left Arrow to jump back to the previous word. NVDA will announce it.
steps.hint: Hold Control and press Left Arrow.
hints: Hold Control and press the Left Arrow. This jumps your cursor back to the start of the previous word.
NVDA announces the word you land on each time. Use this to review words you just passed over.
Control plus Left and Right Arrow together give you fast scanning through any document — much quicker than moving one character at a time.
steps.successMessage: You jumped back one word. Control plus arrows give you fast word-by-word navigation through any document.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Down Arrow moves to the next line in a document. Up Arrow moves to the previous line. NVDA reads the entire line each time you move to it. Sighted users can scan lines visually; you move to each one and NVDA reads it aloud. This is one of the most important navigation methods for documents and web pages. Press Enter to continue.
hints: Down Arrow moves to the next line and NVDA reads the entire line aloud. Up Arrow moves to the previous line.
The Down and Up Arrow keys are in the arrow key cluster in the lower right area of the keyboard.
Line-by-line navigation is the most common way to read through a document, email, or web page — you will use it constantly.

steps.instruction: Arrow into the practice text below, then press Down Arrow to move to the next line. NVDA will read the entire line for you.
steps.hint: The Down Arrow key is in the arrow key cluster. Press it once to move one line at a time.
hints: The Down Arrow key is in the arrow key cluster at the lower right of the keyboard. Press it once to move down one line.
NVDA reads the entire line as you land on it. If you want to hear just part of it again, use NVDA plus Up Arrow to re-read the current line.
Move down a few lines, then try moving back up with the Up Arrow. NVDA reads each line as you move to it in either direction.
steps.successMessage: You moved down one line. NVDA read the entire line for you.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Now press Up Arrow to move back to the previous line. NVDA will read it.
steps.hint: The Up Arrow key is directly above the Down Arrow key.
hints: The Up Arrow key is directly above the Down Arrow key in the arrow cluster. Press it once to move up one line.
NVDA reads the entire line as you move up to it, just as it did when you moved down.
Up and Down Arrow together are your main reading tools for any document. Most screen reader users spend the majority of their time using just these two keys.
steps.successMessage: You moved up one line. Up and Down Arrow are your main tools for reading through any document line by line.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Control+Home jumps immediately to the very beginning of a document, list, or web page. Control+End jumps to the very end. These are great for getting your bearings when you land in a new document. Sighted users can scroll quickly to get the full picture; these keys do the equivalent instantly for keyboard users. Press Enter to continue.
hints: Control plus Home jumps to the very beginning of a document. Control plus End jumps to the very end.
The Home key is usually near the Insert, Delete, and Page Up keys — above the arrow keys on most keyboards.
These two shortcuts are useful whenever you open a new document and want to start reading from the beginning, or jump to the end to see how long it is.

steps.instruction: Arrow into the practice text below, then press Control+Home to jump to the very beginning. NVDA will read the first line.
steps.hint: Hold Control and press the Home key. Home is usually near the Insert, Delete, and Page Up keys.
hints: Hold Control and press the Home key. Home is in the cluster above the arrow keys on most keyboards, near Insert and Delete.
NVDA reads the first line of the document after you jump there. You are now at the very beginning.
Control plus Home works in documents, web pages, lists, and most other text areas. It always takes you to the very top.
steps.successMessage: You jumped to the start. NVDA read the first line.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Now press Control+End to jump to the very last line. NVDA will read it.
steps.hint: Hold Control and press the End key, which is near the Home key.
hints: Hold Control and press the End key. End is near the Home key, in the cluster above the arrow keys.
NVDA reads the last line of the document after you jump there with Control plus End.
Control plus Home and Control plus End together let you quickly get the full scope of any document — jump to the start to begin, jump to the end to see where it finishes.
steps.successMessage: You jumped to the end. Control plus Home or End instantly moves you to either end of any document.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Say All tells NVDA to start reading from the current position to the end of the document without stopping. The keystroke depends on your keyboard layout. If you are on the desktop layout, press NVDA plus Down Arrow. If you are on the laptop layout, press NVDA plus A. NVDA keeps reading until it reaches the end or you stop it. Sighted users can skim an entire page at a glance; Say All is your equivalent — let NVDA read the whole thing while you listen. You can press Control at any time to stop. Press Enter to continue.
hints: Desktop layout: hold your NVDA key and press Down Arrow. Laptop layout: hold your NVDA key and press the letter A. Not sure which layout you are using? Check in the Customizing NVDA chapter.
Press Control at any time to stop Say All immediately. Press Shift to pause it; press Shift again to resume from where it stopped.
Say All is most useful for reading long documents, emails, or web articles without having to press Down Arrow repeatedly.

steps.instruction: Arrow down into the practice text area below and position your cursor somewhere in the middle. Then start Say All: desktop layout users press NVDA plus Down Arrow; laptop layout users press NVDA plus A. NVDA will begin reading everything from your cursor to the end without stopping. Press Control to stop it at any time. When done, press Enter to continue.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.
hints: Desktop layout: hold NVDA — Insert or Caps Lock — and press the Down Arrow key. Laptop layout: hold NVDA and press the letter A.
Once Say All starts, you do not need to do anything — just listen. Press Control to stop at any point.
Try starting Say All from different positions in the practice text. It always reads from the cursor position to the end.

steps.instruction: You can select text by holding the Shift key while pressing arrow keys. As you select, NVDA reads the characters, words, or lines being added to the selection. Sighted users can see highlighted text; NVDA announces each piece you select so you always know exactly what is included. Selected text can be copied with Control+C and pasted with Control+V anywhere you can type. Press Enter to continue.
hints: Hold Shift and press any arrow key to extend a selection. Shift plus Right Arrow selects one character; Shift plus Down Arrow selects one line; Control plus Shift plus Right Arrow selects one word.
NVDA announces each character, word, or line as you add it to the selection. Press any arrow key without Shift to deselect.
Once text is selected, Control plus C copies it and Control plus V pastes it. These are Windows commands that work in every application.

steps.instruction: Arrow into the practice text below. Hold Shift and press Right Arrow to select one character forward. NVDA will announce it. Keep pressing Shift+Right Arrow to extend the selection character by character.
steps.hint: Hold Shift first, then press Right Arrow. Keep Shift held to extend the selection further.
hints: Hold the Shift key first, then press Right Arrow while keeping Shift held. The Shift key is the wide key above the left Control key.
NVDA announces each character as you add it to the selection. You should hear the character name followed by 'selected'.
Keep Shift held and keep pressing Right Arrow to select more characters one at a time. Release Shift when you are done selecting.
steps.successMessage: You selected a character. NVDA announced it as selected.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Now try selecting a whole word. Press Control+Shift+Right Arrow to select the next word at once. NVDA will read the selected word.
steps.hint: Hold both Control and Shift, then press Right Arrow. All three keys together select one word at a time.
hints: Hold Control and Shift at the same time, then press Right Arrow. Control plus Shift plus Right Arrow selects one full word at once.
NVDA announces the selected word. You can keep pressing Control plus Shift plus Right Arrow to select additional words one at a time.
This combination — Control plus Shift plus Right Arrow — is one of the fastest ways to select text. You can build up a multi-word selection quickly.
steps.successMessage: You selected a full word. Holding Shift with any navigation key extends the selection by that amount.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: Now hold Shift and press Down Arrow to select the entire current line and one more below it. NVDA will read the selected block.
steps.hint: Hold Shift and press Down Arrow once to select to the next line.
hints: Hold Shift and press the Down Arrow once. This extends the selection downward by one full line.
NVDA reads the content of the newly selected line as you add it to the selection.
Shift plus Down Arrow is the fastest way to select complete lines. Each additional press adds another line to the selection.
steps.successMessage: You selected a line. Shift plus Down Arrow is great for selecting whole lines quickly.
steps.practiceText:
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Screen readers open the digital world to everyone.
NVDA is free and open source software.
Practice builds the habit of screen reader navigation.
Arrow keys move through text one step at a time.
Control plus arrows jump by whole words at once.
Home and End move to a line boundary.
Control plus Home or End jumps to document extremes.
This is the last line of the practice area.

steps.instruction: To deselect, press any arrow key without Shift. To copy the selection, press Control+C. To paste it somewhere, press Control+V. Selection works the same way in documents, emails, text fields, and web pages wherever NVDA is active. Press Enter to continue.
hints: Press any arrow key without Shift to deselect. Control plus C copies the selection; Control plus V pastes it anywhere you can type.
Control plus A selects all the text in the current document or text area at once — a quick way to select everything.
Selection with Shift plus arrows works identically in Word, Notepad, email, web forms, and most other text areas.

steps.instruction: Excellent work — you have completed the Reading and Moving Through Text chapter! You now know how to navigate by character, word, and line, jump to the start and end of a document, use Say All (NVDA plus Down Arrow on desktop layout, NVDA plus A on laptop layout), and select text with Shift plus arrow keys. These skills work everywhere NVDA is active. Quick tip: if NVDA is reading too fast or too slowly as you practice, you can adjust speech speed and other voice properties on the fly using the synth settings ring — that is covered in the Customizing NVDA chapter. When you are ready, press NVDA+Shift+C to open the lesson picker and start the next chapter: Browse Mode and Web Navigation. Press Enter to finish.
hints: The next chapter is Browse Mode and Web Navigation. It covers how to read and interact with web pages using NVDA's single-letter shortcuts.
If NVDA's reading speed feels too fast or slow, you can adjust it in the Customizing NVDA chapter using the synth settings ring.
NVDA Coach saves your progress. When you return, your completed lessons are marked so you can pick up right where you left off.
